title: Quercus gomeziana
taxonomy: Quercus gomeziana is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Quercus, which includes various oak species. Quercus gomeziana is native to Mexico and Central America, and its classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other oak species. The species is further divided into various subspecies and varieties, which are recognized based on their geographic distribution and morphological features. As a member of the Fagaceae family, Quercus gomeziana shares similarities with other oak species in terms of its leaf morphology and reproductive structures.

intro: Quercus gomeziana, also known as the Gómez oak, is a species of tree in the beech family Fagaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The Gómez oak has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Quercus gomeziana include tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Gómez oak is often used to make tea, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies and supplements in the form of extracts and capsules.
